purpose: Predeclare the smallest task/map outcome probe required after the SLAMOnlyRich revision contract and before any revised SLAMOnlyRich utility formula.

probe_question.fact: Existing source audit and strict edge proxy rows are available for the same 50 request groups, but the current SLAMOnlyRich score is underpowered.
probe_question.agent_inference: The next probe should test whether map/pose evidence has measurable outcome value when separated from semantic confidence, before changing the SLAMOnlyRich formula.
probe_question.paper_claim: This contract is not an ObjectNav, SLAM, SemanticSLAM complementarity, or Step 4-5 benefit claim.

policies.name: SLAMOnlyRich_current
policies.role: current independent map/pose baseline before formula revision

map_side_metrics.name: pose_graph_connectivity_delta
map_side_metrics.definition: Compare each policy against NoReobserveReference using strict edge variants; positive evidence is lower connected component count, higher largest component fraction, higher spatial/loop edge count, or higher mean degree without candidate-overlap-only evidence.

map_side_metrics.name: map_pose_consistency_delta
map_side_metrics.definition: Measure whether the selected re-observation adds non-overlapping pose/depth/map context rather than only repeating the same candidate support.

task_side_metrics.name: wrong_goal_visit_proxy_delta
task_side_metrics.definition: Evaluation-only proxy for whether the selected evidence would reduce wrong semantic goal commitment relative to NoReobserveReference and SemanticOnly. It must be computed from separated labels after action-time decisions are fixed.

task_side_metrics.name: wasted_path_proxy_delta
task_side_metrics.definition: Evaluation-only proxy for whether map/pose-informed re-observation reduces avoidable travel to wrong or low-value evidence after charging re-observation travel cost.

bridge_metrics.name: map_task_alignment_rate
bridge_metrics.definition: Fraction of evaluable rows where map-side delta is positive and task-side proxy is non-worse than SemanticOnly.

bridge_metrics.name: slam_independent_value_rows
bridge_metrics.definition: Rows where SLAMOnlyRich_current or the future revised SLAM-only policy improves map-side metric without using semantic score.

forbidden_action_inputs: candidate overlap as independent pose evidence

failure_taxonomy.meaning: Strict map/pose metrics do not improve over NoReobserveReference or SemanticOnly.
failure_taxonomy.what_failure_teaches: The current source may not expose a useful SLAM-side re-observation signal.

failure_taxonomy.meaning: Map-side metrics improve, but wrong-goal or wasted-path proxy is worse or unavailable.
failure_taxonomy.what_failure_teaches: Pose graph connectivity alone is not enough to justify navigation utility.

failure_taxonomy.meaning: SemanticOnly still dominates all useful rows after the outcome metrics are attached.
failure_taxonomy.what_failure_teaches: The method should remain semantic-only or the SLAM utility needs a different observable.

failure_taxonomy.meaning: Any map-side benefit is erased by re-observation travel cost.
failure_taxonomy.what_failure_teaches: The active SLAM utility needs a reachability-aware or budgeted objective.

failure_taxonomy.meaning: The same 50 request groups are too object-ambiguity-heavy and do not contain enough map-sensitive cases.
failure_taxonomy.what_failure_teaches: A separate split contract is required before changing the request pool.

failure_taxonomy.meaning: Map/pose metrics are saturated or weak on most rows, as in the prior SLAMOnlyRich diagnostic.
failure_taxonomy.what_failure_teaches: Outcome metrics must be refined before revising the SLAM utility formula.

failure_taxonomy.meaning: Evaluation-only labels appear in action-time decisions.
failure_taxonomy.what_failure_teaches: The implementation is invalid and must be fixed before interpretation.

interpretation.fact: This contract defines map-side and task-side outcome metrics, required baselines, allowed and forbidden inputs, output schema, and failure taxonomy for the next probe.
interpretation.agent_inference: The most defensible next engineering step is to implement this probe in Docker and inspect whether strict map/pose evidence aligns with task-side wrong-goal and wasted-path proxies before any utility formula revision.
interpretation.paper_claim: No final H001, ObjectNav, SLAM, or SemanticSLAM contribution claim is allowed from this contract alone.
